events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a datum scheme misunderstanding where a part was checked off the wrong surface (DOC - Datum of Control?). This is a GD&T error where the inspector checked the part against the wrong datum surface, leading to incorrect acceptance of a potentially non-compliant part.

Key points:
- Datum scheme misunderstood - part checked off wrong surface
- Work is rough-complete (installed/assembled but not closed out)
- This is a non-compliant condition - the part may have been accepted incorrectly
- The defect is that the datum reference frame was not properly applied

Decision: fail - because the part was checked against wrong datum, meaning inspection results are invalid and the part's actual compliance is unknown. This is non-compliant.

Risk: high - because parts could be accepted that don't meet spec, leading to assembly issues.

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant

Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 70%

Findings: datum scheme misunderstood, part checked against wrong surface, inspection results invalid

Actions: re-inspect against correct datum scheme, quarantine affected parts, retrain on datum reference frame per ASME Y14.5

References: ASME Y14.5, gauge instructions
